objective: Prototype single-instance exclusion, durable journal intent and reference-counted power activity behavior.
deliverables: Kernel-backed single-writer guard and second-instance zero-side-effect fixture.
Append-only durable journal intent/checkpoint prototype with fault injection.
Reference-counted power activity lease with release coverage for success, failure, cancellation and throw.

verification.expectedEvidence: The external process count is zero and the Job enters an explicit failure/recovery state.
Only one writer exists; the second process creates no Job, HDC access or Session write.
Exactly one process owns the writer lock across all contention cases.

verification.method: Filesystem durability and torn-write fault matrix.
verification.expectedEvidence: Failed durable intent prevents the command and torn/invalid journal tails are detectable.

verification.method: Reference-counted activity tests and idle-sleep observation.
verification.expectedEvidence: The lease is active only during the critical scope and is released on every terminal path; limits are documented.

stopConditions: The second instance can reach HDC or write Session state before lock ownership is established.
A journal sync failure can still dispatch an external-effect step.
Power activity cannot be released deterministically on every exit path.
